questions.stem: Using the modified Dulong formula $HHV = 337C + 1419(H - O/8) + 93S$ (kJ/kg), a fuel with $C=45\%$, $H=6\%$, $O=40\%$, $S=0\%$ has a higher heating value of _____ kJ/kg.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Apply the modified Dulong formula with percentages.

**Working.**
$$337(45)+1419(6-40/8)+93(0)=16584\,\text{kJ/kg}.$$

**Answer.** $16584\,\text{kJ/kg}$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: In the integrated solid-waste-management hierarchy, the most preferred option is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The hierarchy ranks: reduce > reuse > recycle > recover (energy) > dispose (landfill).

**Working.**
Source reduction.

**Answer.** Source reduction

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Which characteristics define a hazardous waste under typical regulatory frameworks?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The four hazard characteristics are ignitability, corrosivity, reactivity and toxicity; moisture content is not one.

**Working.**
Ignitability, corrosivity, reactivity.

**Answer.** Ignitability, corrosivity, reactivity

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: The optimum carbon-to-nitrogen (C/N) ratio for efficient aerobic composting is approximately
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A C/N around 25–30:1 balances microbial energy (C) and protein synthesis (N).

**Working.**
≈ 25–30:1.

**Answer.** 25–30:1
